data.title: Snowflake in spotlight as TD Cowen sees 'constructive' partner checks ahead of Q4 results
data.description:
Snowflake (NYSE:SNOW) was in the spotlight on Friday as TD Cowen said recent partner checks were “constructive” ahead of the data processing and warehouse company's fourth-quarter results next week.

Shares were little changed in premarket trading.

“Our partner checks were constructive, citing growth stability in core SQL workloads, increasing traction w/ new products (especially Cortex AI Search) & unstructured data, positive market reception around Iceberg and improving GTM motions (engaging w/ more tech users),” TD Cowen analysts wrote in a note to clients. “Expecting solid 4Q out-performance & see room for FY26 numbers to move higher.”

The analysts reiterated their Buy rating on Snowflake and upped their price target to $210 from $190.

Looking to the fourth-quarter, the analysts said one partner saw its Snowflake business grow 29% in the period, above its target range of 25% to 28%. It is also targeting roughly 30% growth in fiscal 2026, with areas like larger scale cloud modernization, increased traction with Snowpark and Cortez, “AI firepower” and added flexibility. There was also an improvement in Snowflake's go-to-market initiatives, with “greater involvement” from tech buyers.

“We look for upside returning to [trailing 12-month average] levels (~3.5%) vs. high-end of guide at ~23%, and we note there was likely extra holiday/OOO seasonality this [quarter] (as was expected),” the analysts wrote. “We think Street at 22% growth for FY26 is conservative (we model 23%) and see room for numbers to be walked up.”

A consensus of analysts expect Snowflake to earn $0.18 per share on $955.52M in revenue.

data.title: Dividend Roundup: Delta Air Lines, Goldman Sachs, Occidental Petroleum, Nuecor, and more
data.description:
This week's dividend activity included increased payouts from Waste Management (WM) and Occidental Petroleum (NYSE:OXY) as well as declarations from companies such as Nuecor (NYSE:NUE) and M&T Bank (MTB).

Additionally, looking towards next week, industry players such as Delta Air Lines (NYSE:DAL) and Goldman Sachs (NYSE:GS) will see the ex-dividend dates for their upcoming dividend payments.

See a breakdown below of some of the highlights of this week, along with some upcoming activity on the dividend front.

Dividend Increases

Here are some companies that have announced a dividend increase in the past week:

Waste Management (WM)

  • Dividend declared: February 17
  • New dividend amount: $0.825
  • Previous amount: $0.75
  • Increase: 10%

Sherwin Williams (SHW)

  • Dividend declared: February 19
  • New dividend amount: $0.79
  • Previous amount: $0.7149
  • Increase: 10.5%

Occidental Petroleum (NYSE:OXY)

  • Dividend declared: February 20
  • New dividend amount: $0.24
  • Previous amount: $0.22
  • Increase: 9.1%

Dividend Declarations

Here are some companies that have announced a dividend in the past week:

Whirlpool (WHR)

  • Dividend declared: February 17
  • Dividend amount: $1.75

Nucor (NYSE:NUE)

  • Dividend declared: February 18
  • Dividend amount: $0.55

M&T Bank (MTB)

  • Dividend declared: February 19
  • Dividend amount: $1.35

Agilent Technologies (A)

  • Dividend declared: February 19
  • Dividend amount: $0.248

Upcoming Dividend Calendar

Here are some companies that have an ex-dividend date coming next week:

Phillips 66 (PSX)

  • Ex-dividend date: February 24
  • Payout date: March 5

S&P Global (SPGI)

  • Ex-dividend date: February 26
  • Payout date: March 12

Delta Air Lines (NYSE:DAL)

  • Ex-dividend date: February 27
  • Payout date: March 20

Goldman Sachs (NYSE:GS)

  • Ex-dividend date: February 28
  • Payout date: March 28

data.title: Credissential signs LoI to acquire CoinCmply
data.description:
• Credissential (OTCPK:IPTNF) said on Friday that it has signed a non-binding letter of intent to acquire CoinCmply, a private company specializing in cryptocurrency tax compliance solutions for Canadian and United States crypto users.
  • CoinCmply is a platform designed to assist cryptocurrency users in managing and simplifying their tax obligations.
  • Under the terms of the LOI, at closing of the transaction, the company will issue an aggregate of 13.6M common shares in the company at a deemed price of $0.08 per consideration share to the shareholders of CoinCmply pro rata to their respective shareholdings in CoinCmply.
  • The proposed transaction is considered arm’s length. The company does not expect to assume any long-term debt in connection with the transaction.
  • This acquisition is expected to enhance Credissential's capabilities in providing AI-driven financial solutions, particularly in the areas of identity protection, credit building, budgeting, and asset optimization.

data.title: Dow futures fall sharply as DoJ's probe on UnitedHealth weighs on the index
data.description:
Stock index futures were mixed on Friday, as markets look to regain some momentum after Thursday's sell-off, which was partly driven by Walmart's disappointing outlook.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) +0.2%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +0.3%, and Dow futures (INDU) -0.5%.

Dow futures' tumbled, in part driven by UnitedHealth's (UNH) ~12% drop in the premarket, after The Wall Street Journal reported that the U.S. Department of Justice has launched a new investigation into the company’s business practices.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was down 2 basis points to 4.48%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 1 basis point to 4.27%.

U.S. stocks finished with losses Thursday, with the S&P 500 pulling back from a record high, in part as investors balked at Walmart's (WMT) soft financial outlook.

"We saw a moderate risk-off move yesterday, with the S&P 500 falling back from its all-time high," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

The main story in the U.S. was a weaker-than-expected forecast from Walmart, which added to nerves about the health of the consumer right now, especially after a soft retail sales print last week, Reid added.

On the economic side, the February PMI composite flash and January existing home sales are due during market hours. The latter is expected to come in at 4.13M.

The February consumer sentiment is forecasted to come in at 67.8.

"Final February U.S. Michigan consumer sentiment data is mainly of use as a source of entertainment, such is the degree of political partisanship," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

However, approval for U.S. President Donald Trump’s economic policies and Trump mega-donor Musk has been falling, and the Michigan sentiment may hint at whether that disapproval is partisan or not, Donovan added.
